channel.title: non-fiction on rahuldevajji
channel.description: Recent content in non-fiction on rahuldevajji

item.title: The Diary of Frida Kahlo: An Intimate Self-Portrait
item.description: The introductions are dense, but once you get through them you can peer into Frida Kahlo’s mind. Seeing her artistic process and raw sketches is wonderful!. Some of her drafts would probably be works of art even today.

item.title: A Cloud a Day
item.description: This book is delightful! It made me look up at the sky more. It’s a perfect road‑trip companion, because it will keep you occupied trying to identify everything you see in the sky.

item.title: Free Will
item.description: Okay, I read this book in almost 6 months ago (at the time of writing this review on 03/2026). Looking back on it, I feel I learnt nothing. The author argues put together some shower thoughts, cited some old research, and called it done. I guess it is pretty short because he doesn’t have enough things to say about this topic, it is even more evident based on how he decides to end the book “I’m hungry, yes, but it also seems that I’ve made my point.
